source: Travis D's FAR Clause Guide — SIO internal
note: Initial seed of the most commonly encountered FAR/CAR/DFARS flow-down clauses in SIO sponsor contracts, classified Green / Red / Grey. Extend by adding entries below; the Zod schema and guide-linkage test will catch typos and broken FAR section references.

entries.title: Definitions
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Standard definitions clause with no substantive flow-down impact.

entries.title: Gratuities
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Prohibits gratuities to government employees. Standard institutional policy already complies.

entries.title: Covenant Against Contingent Fees
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC does not use contingent-fee arrangements for federal awards.

entries.title: Anti-Kickback Procedures
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Conforms to UC policy on procurement ethics.

entries.title: Limitation on Payments to Influence Certain Federal Transactions
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC complies with the Byrd Anti-Lobbying Amendment; appropriated funds are not used for lobbying.

entries.title: Contractor Code of Business Ethics and Conduct
entries.guidance: Conditional. UC has an institutional code of ethics; accept where the contract value + period triggers the clause. Confirm the PI-facing training requirement is scoped to key personnel.
entries.question_for_pi: Will any key personnel require additional ethics training beyond UC's standard compliance curriculum?

entries.title: Basic Safeguarding of Covered Contractor Information Systems
entries.guidance: Conditional. Acceptable only if the project processes, stores, or transmits Federal Contract Information (FCI). Fundamental research without FCI should not trigger this clause; if the sponsor asserts it applies, verify with the PI and ITS.
entries.question_for_pi: Will the project handle any non-public information the Government provides or that is generated for the Government under this contract?
notes: Applies only to systems that handle FCI.
If CUI is involved, NIST 800-171 / 52.204-27 / DFARS 252.204-7012 likely attach and require separate review.

entries.title: Prohibition on Contracting for Hardware, Software, and Services Developed or Provided by Kaspersky Lab
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC does not procure or deploy Kaspersky products on research systems.

entries.title: Prohibition on Contracting for Certain Telecommunications and Video Surveillance Services or Equipment
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC complies with Section 889. Representation is handled institutionally.

entries.title: Protecting the Government's Interest When Subcontracting With Contractors Debarred, Suspended, or Proposed for Debarment
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Standard SAM.gov exclusion check is performed before subawards.

entries.title: Contract Terms and Conditions—Commercial Products and Commercial Services
entries.guidance: Remove or replace. 52.212-4 is drafted for commercial-item procurements and carries inspection, warranty, and termination-for-convenience terms that are inappropriate for a cost-reimbursement sponsored-research agreement at a non-profit educational institution.
entries.suggested_justification: Scripps is a non-profit educational research institution and cannot accept commercial-item terms (inspection system, warranty of merchantability, unlimited termination-for-convenience liability) for fundamental research. Request deletion or substitution with the appropriate research-agreement terms.

alt_guidance.note: Time-and-materials / labor-hour variant — also inappropriate for cost-reimbursement research.

entries.title: Audit and Records—Negotiation
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC complies with federal audit and records retention requirements under 2 CFR 200.

entries.title: Utilization of Small Business Concerns
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC maintains a small-business utilization program at the institutional level.

entries.title: Small Business Subcontracting Plan
entries.guidance: Conditional. Only applies above the subcontracting-plan threshold; confirm the award amount and whether UC's master small-business plan satisfies the requirement.
entries.question_for_pi: What's the total award value, and is subcontracting anticipated above the clause threshold?

entries.title: Equal Opportunity
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC complies with EO 11246 and maintains affirmative action programs.

entries.title: Equal Opportunity for Veterans
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC complies with VEVRAA.

entries.title: Equal Opportunity for Workers with Disabilities
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC complies with Section 503 of the Rehabilitation Act.

entries.title: Combating Trafficking in Persons
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC has an institutional anti-trafficking policy and monitoring procedure.

entries.title: Restrictions on Certain Foreign Purchases
entries.guidance: Acceptable. UC does not transact with OFAC-sanctioned entities.

entries.title: Authorization and Consent
entries.guidance: Conditional. Generally acceptable for sponsored research; confirm which Alternate is invoked and that it extends authorization and consent to UC's work.

alt_guidance.note: Extends authorization and consent to first-tier subcontractors — preferred for research.

entries.title: Patent Indemnity
entries.guidance: Conditional. Patent indemnification flow-downs to a non-profit research institution should be carefully scoped. Request deletion or scope to UC's actual contribution.
entries.suggested_justification: As a non-profit educational institution, UC cannot accept open-ended patent indemnification. Request deletion or limit to willful infringement.

entries.title: Patent Rights—Ownership by the Contractor
entries.guidance: Acceptable. This is the Bayh-Dole clause and is the preferred IP framework for UC-performed federally funded research.

entries.title: Rights in Data—General
entries.guidance: Conditional. Basic clause is acceptable; Alternates II–V carry additional restrictions that may conflict with UC's fundamental-research and publication posture.

alt_guidance.note: Limited rights in privately-financed data — generally acceptable.
Restricted rights in computer software — review with PI before acceptance.
Withholds deliverable data pending agency review — REMOVE. Conflicts with fundamental-research publication rights.

entries.question_for_pi: Do you anticipate publishing results without prior agency approval? Which Alternate is invoked?

entries.title: Rights in Data—Special Works
entries.guidance: Remove. Gives the Government unlimited rights and can require copyright assignment — incompatible with UC's fundamental-research posture.
entries.suggested_justification: Special-works copyright assignment conflicts with UC's authorship and publication policies. Request substitution with 52.227-14 Basic.

entries.title: Cost Accounting Standards
entries.guidance: Conditional. UC is subject to educational-institution CAS under 48 CFR 9905. Confirm the award triggers CAS-covered contract thresholds and that the modified-CAS framework for educational institutions applies rather than the full commercial CAS set.
notes: Educational institutions are generally covered by the modified CAS at 48 CFR 9905, not the full 9904 commercial set.

entries.title: Prompt Payment
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Standard payment-terms clause.

entries.title: Unenforceability of Unauthorized Obligations
entries.guidance: Acceptable.

entries.title: Disputes
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Standard Contract Disputes Act clause.

entries.title: Protest After Award
entries.guidance: Acceptable.

entries.title: Stop-Work Order
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Standard Government right to suspend performance.

entries.title: Changes—Fixed-Price
entries.guidance: Acceptable if the contract is fixed-price; confirm contract type before accepting.

entries.title: Changes—Cost-Reimbursement
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Preferred changes clause for cost-reimbursement research.

entries.title: Subcontracts
entries.guidance: Conditional. Confirm which Alternate is invoked; Alternate I requires consent to subcontract that may slow research-driven subaward execution.
entries.question_for_pi: Are any subawards anticipated, and at what anticipated value?

entries.title: Government Property
entries.guidance: Conditional. Acceptable only if government-furnished property is actually contemplated; otherwise request deletion. UC's property management system is approved under UC-wide procedures.
entries.question_for_pi: Will the sponsor provide equipment, materials, or other government property?

entries.title: Limitation of Liability
entries.guidance: Remove. Limitation-of-liability clauses drafted for commercial supplies do not fit fundamental research and can conflict with UC's risk posture.
entries.suggested_justification: UC is a non-profit educational institution performing fundamental research. Request deletion; standard sponsored-research liability terms apply.

entries.title: Termination for Convenience of the Government (Fixed-Price)
entries.guidance: Conditional. Acceptable for fixed-price awards; preserve the contractor's right to settlement of allowable costs incurred.

entries.title: Termination (Cost-Reimbursement)
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Preferred termination clause for cost-reimbursement research.

entries.title: Excusable Delays
entries.guidance: Acceptable. Standard force-majeure framework.

entries.title: Compliance with the Laws
entries.guidance: Remove. The Department of Commerce CAR clause requires contractor compliance representations that extend beyond UC's institutional compliance posture.
entries.suggested_justification: UC complies with all applicable laws through institutional policy; a contract-specific attestation is duplicative.

entries.title: Foreign National Visitors and Guests
entries.guidance: Remove. Imposes visitor-screening obligations incompatible with UC's open-campus fundamental-research environment.
entries.suggested_justification: UC operates an open research campus under NSDD-189. Foreign-national visitor screening beyond export-control requirements is not compatible with fundamental research.

entries.title: Safeguarding Covered Defense Information and Cyber Incident Reporting
entries.guidance: Remove unless the project actually handles Covered Defense Information (CDI/CUI). For fundamental research with no CUI, the clause should not attach; if the sponsor asserts it does, request a scope letter and engage ITS and the Export Control Office before accepting.
entries.suggested_justification: Fundamental research at Scripps does not generate or handle CDI. If the sponsor confirms CDI will be provided, accepting this clause requires NIST SP 800-171 compliance at the system level — treat as a scope change.
entries.question_for_pi: Will the project receive any information marked CUI, CDI, or ITAR from the sponsor?

entries.title: NIST SP 800-171 DoD Assessment Requirements
entries.guidance: Conditional. Tied to 252.204-7012. If that clause is removed, this one should be removed too. If 7012 remains, confirm the required DoD assessment is in SPRS for the covered system.

entries.title: Rights in Technical Data—Noncommercial Items
entries.guidance: Conditional. Acceptable for DoD-funded research provided unlimited rights are scoped to actual deliverables and UC retains publication rights consistent with fundamental research.
entries.question_for_pi: What technical data deliverables are contemplated, and will any be developed exclusively at private expense?
